1998-08-01.title: Military Spending Approved With Curbs on Rights Abuses
1998-08-01.abstract: Senate, 97-2, approves $252-billion military spending bill that includes Sen Patrick Leahy's amendment barring Pentagon from conducting training missions with foreign forces that have record of human rights abuses; training of Indonesian commandos noted; bill includes $1.9 billion for open-ended US mission in Bosnia, but Senate rejects bipartisan effort to reduce US military presence in region to 5,000 by Oct 1999; Senate also requires Pres Clinton to consult Congress before committing any forces to Kosovo; spending on new weapons would rise to about $84.8 billion, from $79.5 billion; military pay would increase 3.6 percent next year (S)

1998-08-01.title: Serbs Say Kosovo Drive Is Over, But Little Changes on the Ground
1998-08-01.abstract: Kosovo has thousands of new refugees fleeing Serb attacks out of sight of diplomats and foreign monitors, day after Serb Government decrees end to military offensive in mostly-Albanian province; shelling in one village described; UN estimates 50,000 people are on the road, in addition to thousands more displaced earlier; photo; map(M)

1998-08-01.title: Japan's New Chief Vows to Act on Economy
1998-08-01.abstract: Japan's new Prime Min, Keizo Obuchi, promises to move swiftly to address country's economic problems; admits his naivete on economic matters, but names Toyoo Gyohten, legendary financier, as special adviser; photo bowing to Emperor Akihito; unemployment in June rose to record 4.3 percent (S)

1998-08-01.title: Biggest German Bank Admits and Regrets Dealing in Nazi Gold
1998-08-01.abstract: Deutsche Bank AG, biggest bank in Germany, admits dealing in Nazi gold and 'regrets' injustices that occurred; declines to say whether wartime officers knew that some of gold may have come from concentration camp victims; independent report commissioned by bank has found that bank channeled gold through branches in Austria and Turkey; 744 of 4,446 gold kilograms consisted of dental gold from Jews, wedding bands and other jewelry amassed in Berlin by SS officer; findings strength class-action suit by Holocaust survivors against Deutsche and Dresdner Bank (S)

1998-08-01.title: India and Pakistan Fail To Narrow Differences
1998-08-01.abstract: Effort to open regular talks between India and Pakistan collapses in acrimony over procedures; sticking point as usual is Kashmir (S)

1998-08-02.title: A Group Uproots Arabs to Plant Jews
1998-08-02.abstract: Jewish National Fund is embroiled in controversy over its efforts to settle Jews in Arab neighborhoods of East Jerusalem; Israeli peace activists assail its plans to evict Palestinian family of 28 from their home of 32 years and hand it over to Jewish settlers; fund says it is carrying out a mandate to acquire property for Jews in their ancient homeland; photo; map (M)

1998-08-02.title: James Lighthill, 74, an Innovative Mathematician
1998-08-02.abstract: Sir James Lighthill, innovative British mathematician whose ideas about aerodynamics influenced design of supersonic Concorde, died on July 17 while he was trying to swim around Isle of Sark in English Channel; he was 74; cause of death was heart failure (M)

1998-08-02.title: Perplexing Formula Clinches Slim Majority for Cambodian Chief
1998-08-02.abstract: Hun Sun's Cambodian People's Party is expected to secure a majority in Parliament thanks to a complex formula that favors larger parties like his over smaller ones; will still lack the two-thirds' majority needed to form a government; Hun Sen has offered to form government with chief rivals, Prince Norodom Ranariddh and Sam Rainsy, but they are still protesting vote count itself (M)

1998-08-02.title: I.M.F. Official in Moscow Presses Kremlin on Reform
1998-08-02.abstract: Stanley Fischer, deputy managing director of International Monetary Fund, visits Moscow and says Russian Government has so far been keeping commitments made in July to win $22.6 billion in IMF credits; cites important progress in critical area of tax revenues; warns, however, that fund's board will be watching Government's actions over next few weeks and months to see if Russia qualifies in September for its next $4.3 billion credit (M)

1998-08-02.title: It's Kenya's Farmers vs. Wildlife, and the Animals Are Losing
1998-08-02.abstract: Kenya's wild animals are threatened by country's farmers, who for 20 years have been encroaching on lands that were once wild and who now regard wild animals as pests; the 8 percent of Kenya's land that is set for wild animals cannot accommodate their migration; at least 40 percent of range animals have disappeared from Kenya's savannahs since 1977, when hunting was made illegal, and they are continuing to disappear at rate of 2 percent to 3 percent a year; ecological debacle sets off strenuous debate among ranchers, scientists, conservationists and small farmers; stakes are high for Kenya, since wildlife brings in about $400 million a year in tourist revenue; photos; map (M)

1998-08-03.title: End of Line for Mob's Shuttle Over the Hong Kong Border
1998-08-03.abstract: Chinese police disbands brazen gang of mobsters that has operated in Hong Kong for years, committing robberies, kidnappings and murders and fleeing across border to hide in Guangzhou, where local police have been either too inept or corrupt to care; arrests ringleader, Cheung Tze-keung, and 32 accomplices; seize nearly $5 million, as well as 11 luxury cars and cache of explosives and firearms; gang's downfall is latest chapter in curious evolution of lawlessness that straddles border--area rich with history of secret societies, pirates and criminal intrigue; photo; map (M)

1998-08-03.title: Russia Starts To Feel Pain As Miners Block Rails
1998-08-03.abstract: Strikes by Russian coal miners, who have blocked rail lines for months, are being felt on Sakhalin Island in Russian Far East, where officials say they may be forced to cut off electricity for 24 hours in next several days to all but essential users; already, many of island's 720,000 residents are without power for as much as 11 hours per day; map (M)

1998-08-03.title: Bulgaria Becomes a Weapons Bazaar
1998-08-03.abstract: World of Arms, ongoing series on spread of arms, says Bulgaria, former supplier of weapons for Soviet Union, has become arms bazaar for rebels and terrorist organizations worldwide; country's arms manufacturers, having lost traditional cold war markets, are desperate for customers, whom they accommodate with false shipping documents to disguise both buyer and destinations; at center of arms trade are Kintex, state marketing agency, and Arsenal, country's largest arms manufacturer, which is also state-owned; the big demand is for is Kalashnikov assault rifle; intelligence and industry experts say supply of weapons fueling regional wars could be significantly reduced if Bulgarian arms traffic were cleaned up or shut down; US and European Union, while voicing concern, have done little to stop trade (M)

1998-08-03.title: Karl Schirdewan, 91, Maverick Among East German Officials
1998-08-03.abstract: Karl Schirdewan, high-ranking figure in old East German Communist Government, dies at age 91 (S)

1998-08-03.title: Global Arms Bazaar Booming in Bulgaria
1998-08-03.abstract: Once the renowned supplier of weapons for the Soviet Union, Bulgaria has become the arms bazaar for rebels and terrorist organizations of every political, ethnic and religious persuasion, Western officials and experts say.    If the Bulgarian arms traffic were shut down or cleaned up, the supply of weapons fueling regional wars would be significantly reduced, intelligence and industry experts say.

1998-08-04.title: Cuban Exile Says He Lied to Times About Financial Support
1998-08-04.abstract: Luis Posada Carriles, Cuban exile who conducted violent operations against Cuban Government, says he deliberately misinformed New York Times in series of recent interviews that leaders of Cuban-American National Foundation--most influential exile organization in US--had provided him with financial support; says he lied because Times had information about financial backers in US whom he wished to protect; says he misdirected newspaper by asserting that he received money from Jorge Mas Canosa, who, until death in 1997, was chairman of foundation (M)

1998-08-04.title: NATO Approval Renews Threat of Force in Kosovo
1998-08-04.abstract: US says NATO has approved plans for use of military force in separatist crisis in Serbian province of Kosovo; plans reportedly center on use of air power, rather than ground troops; announcement is intended to push Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic to end current offensive against ethnic Albanians (M)

1998-08-04.title: Leftists Challenge Election Loss in a Mexican State
1998-08-04.abstract: Leftist party in southern Mexican state of Oaxaca challenges preliminary returns day after election, which show its candidate for governor, Andres Manuel Lopez Obrador, ran second; says balloting was marred by fraud (S)

1998-08-04.title: Indonesia Looks Into Disappearance of Protesters
1998-08-04.abstract: Indonesian Govt launches investigation into kidnapping and torture of pro-democracy advocates under former Pres Suharto and possible involvement of his son-in-law and other members of military (S)

1998-08-04.title: Tracing Money, Swiss Outdo U.S. On Mexico Drug Corruption Case
1998-08-04.abstract: When the young chief of Switzerland's drug police arrived here in late 1995 to inquire about $132 million found in Swiss bank accounts belonging to the influential elder brother of a former President, he knew enough about Mexico's political underworld to be wary of his official hosts.    No sooner had he been whisked from the airport by a squad of burly Mexican plainclothesmen, for example, than he began to wonder if he wasn't being kidnapped.

1998-08-05.title: Kidnapping and Death in China Complicate Talks on Taiwan
1998-08-05.abstract: New round of talks between China and Taiwan, originally expected to be held later in year, have been imperiled by kidnapping and murder of Taiwanese legislator Lin Ti-chuan in northeastern China; killing does not appear to be politically motivated; officials in Beijing say vast manhunt is under way to find three suspects (M)

1998-08-05.title: Ties Strained Over Water and a Checkpoint : Malaysia's Leader Fires Hot Words at Singapore
1998-08-05.abstract: KUALA LUMPU—  Relations between Singapore and Malaysia — rocky during the best of times — hit a new low Tuesday as disputes over water supply to the island republic and a railway border checkpoint came to a boil.

1998-08-05.title: U.S. Privatization Move Threatens Agreement to Buy Enriched Uranium From Russia
1998-08-05.abstract: $12 billion deal to turn surplus bomb uranium in Russia into fuel for American nuclear power plants is threatened by recent moves that will cut uranium prices and give Russia less revenue (M)

1998-08-05.title: Thousands of Refugees Flee Serb Mountain Attack
1998-08-05.abstract: Thousands of ethnic Albanian refugees, many of them children, are fleeing Serbian Government forces in Kosovo but finding almost no place to hide in broiling heat; map; photo (M)

1998-08-05.title: Iraqi Rejection Of Scrutiny Brings Rebuke From U.S.
1998-08-05.abstract: White House says Iraq's refusal to cooperate with United Nations arms inspectors will only insure that Baghdad remains under sanctions indefinitely (M)

1998-08-06.title: IRAQIS BREAK OFF ALL COOPERATION WITH INSPECTORS
1998-08-06.abstract: Iraq says it is ending all cooperation with United Nations arms inspectors and demands dismissal of chief inspector Richard Butler through restructuring of his disarmament commission; action breaks agreement made in Feb with Sec Gen Kofi Annan, which averted US and British military action against Iraq; independent international technical evaluation in July, one of number demanded by Iraq, concluded that Baghdad had not met commission's requirements on accounting for biological arms; Iraq also urges that disarmament panel be moved to Geneva or Vienna from New York to free itself of US influence (M)

1998-08-06.title: Doctors Powerless as AIDS Rakes Africa
1998-08-06.abstract: First article in series Dead Zones discusses AIDS epidemic in Zimbabwe; death from every type of disease has become so common in Zimbabwe, as in much of Africa, that it no longer seems to matter that country has become center of world's AIDS epidemic; recent studies suggest that Zimbabwe may have highest AIDS infection rate on earth; savage virus has left few people untouched; it has devastated families and communities; seen through weary eyes of doctors and nurses who must attend to sick people around them, AIDS is just another word for dying, and it is just another disease; with average of $10 to spend on each person's health every year, most African countries have no money for tests, fancy drugs or complicated support networks; Zimbabwe once had health system that was envy of southern Africa, fueled by AIDS and also by increasing poverty and instability, illness has begun to overcome country; maps; photos (M)

1998-08-06.title: Months of Water Rationing Leave a Lot of Throats Parched : Kuala Lumpur's Taps Tapped Out
1998-08-06.abstract: KUALA LUMPUR—  T. Shanmuganathan does not remember the last time he had two consecutive days of running water.

1998-08-06.title: 4 Africans Occupying Nuncio's House Seek to Stay in France
1998-08-06.abstract: Four African immigrants demanding permission to stay in France and five of their supporters have occupied residence of Papal Nuncio in Paris since Aug 1, refusing to leave until Socialist Government of Prime Min Lionel Jospin agrees to give them legal papers (M)

1998-08-06.title: Mexico City Journal; Crime Is Unleashed but the D.A. Is Undaunted
1998-08-06.abstract: Mexico City Dist Atty Samuel Del Villar has made suppression of crime and corruption crusade; his largest achievement so far has been prosecution of International Business Machines on conspiracy charges related to procurement contract; IBM is to pay Mexico City $37.5 million in cash and new computers, which he says will help to modernize city's vast criminal justice bureaucracy; Del Villar photo (M)

1998-08-07.title: Todor Zhivkov Dies at 86; Ruled Bulgaria for 35 Years
1998-08-07.abstract: Todor Zhivkov, Communist ruler of Bulgaria from 1954 until his ouster in 1989, dies at age 86; photo (M)

1998-08-07.title: Americans Fish as Resentful Canadians Observe a Salmon Ban
1998-08-07.abstract: Hundreds of Canadian fishermen in communities that dot British Columbia's rugged coastline are observing Canadian fishing ban on fragile northern coho salmon stocks; they resent inability of Fisheries Min David Anderson to reach agreement with Alaska over coho stocks; Alaskan officials say Canada's fishing ban is extreme reaction (M)

1998-08-07.title: Todi Journal; For a Happy Few, the Hamptons With Castles
1998-08-07.abstract: Medieval city of Todi, Italy, is summer home to growing numbers of wealthy Americans who are lovingly restoring 14th-century fortresses, abbeys and farmhouses, and adding luxury bathrooms, swimming pools and tennis courts; longtime expatriates, old guard who got to Todi first, are unhappy about influx of new and flashier guard that threatens to follow; stringent new zoning law makes it impossible to build new houses or to renovate in anything but time-honored Umbrian style; but old-timers worry that new generation of jet-setters will bring 'Hamptons' style to Todi, complete with catered lunches, cocktail parties and charity balls; photos; map (M)

1998-08-07.title: Congo Rebels Seize Oil Port, Attacking Kabila on 2 Fronts
1998-08-07.abstract: Rebels in Congo seize control of Moanda, strategic oil port in far west, significantly widening their campaign to wrest power from Pres Laurent Kabila; seizure confirms dramatic opening of second front in five-day-old battle that has been largely confined to Congo's far east; Kabila says he is preparing for long, drawn-out war and says ordinary Congolese may be called on to take up arms; map (M)

1998-08-07.title: Accusing Arafat of Inaction, 2 Ministers Resign
1998-08-07.abstract: Two of Yasir Arafat's cabinet ministers resign to protest what they say has been Arafat's failure to address problems within Palestinian Authority; resignations come day after Arafat unveiled long-postponed cabinet shuffle that failed to respond to their calls for him to root out graft and mismanagement; one who resigned, Hanan Ashrawi, is one of most prominent Palestinians on international stage; along with stalled talks on broader peace with Israel, Arafat's chief problems have been worsening economy in Palestinian-ruled areas and nearly universal perception that Authority is corrupt and inefficient (M)

1998-08-08.title: Congo Asks Neighbors to Press Rwanda to Halt Aid to Rebels
1998-08-08.abstract: Pres Laurent Kabila of the Congo, facing mounting rebel threat, meets with other African leaders in effort to convince them to press Rwanda to end its support for six-day-old rebellion; South Africa reaffirms its support for Kabila's rule; map (M)

1998-08-08.title: Few Triumphs for Those Sifting 60 Feet Of Rubble, Listening for a Muffled Voice
1998-08-08.abstract: Rescuers dig through rubble in Nairobi, Kenya, searching for survivors of US Embassy bombing; photos (M)

1998-08-08.title: FOCUS ON SUSPECTS IN PAST ATTACKS
1998-08-08.abstract: Clinton Administration officals say attacks on two US embassies in East Africa only minutes apart probably were carried out by one of a small number of sophisticated terrorist groups presumed to have attacked American installations in the past; Pres Clinton orders teams of FBI agents, medical specialists and explosives experts to scenes of bombings; Under Sec of State Thomas Pickering says it is too early to single out suspects or to speculate about why embassies in Kenya and Tanzania had been targeted; Sec of State Madeleine Albright is reported to be rushing home from Italy; senior US intelligence official, speaking on condition of anonymity, says investigators are focusing on Osama bin Laden, multimillionaire Saudi-born financier who has sworn to wage holy war on United States; Administration officials say there are also suspicions that attacks may have been carried out by Islamic Holy War group in Egypt; photos (M)

1998-08-08.title: New U.N. Push to Urge Iraq To Cooperate With Inspectors
1998-08-08.abstract: Diplomats from Security Council member countries say that countries friendly to Iraq are being asked to try to persuade Government of Pres Saddam Hussein to cooperate with arms inspectors again in return for a new hearing at United Nations (M)

1998-08-08.title: 2 Civilians And G.I. Confirmed As Killed
1998-08-08.abstract: Sgt Kenneth R Hobson 2d, 27-year-old clerical aide to US military attache at US Embassy in Nairobi, is identified as one of American fatalities in bombing; photos (M)

1998-08-09.title: In Europe, New Allure of Air-Conditioning
1998-08-09.abstract: Air conditioning, taken for granted in United States, is catching on in Europe; Europeans, used to air-conditioned cars and workplaces, increasingly want to cool their homes as well; indeed, Europe is becoming a big market for air-conditioning industry; sales of air conditioners for homes and small businesses have grown more than 20 percent over last three years, even as they stayed flat in United States; chart shows sales of air-conditioning equipment, United States and Europe, '95-98; photo (M)

1998-08-09.title: A Challenge To Immunity Of Lobbyists At the U.N.
1998-08-09.abstract: Arundhati Ghose, India's envoy to UN, begins campaign to alert United Nations and Swiss officials to danger of suspected criminals and terrorists taking advantage of legal immunity granted to certain interest groups while in Switzerland on UN business; acts after Indian separatist group took responsibility for kidnapping of her nephew, Sanjoy Ghose, head of private development agency helping villagers in northeast India; his body has never been found; Anup Chetia, leader of United Liberation Front of Assam, which took responsibility for kidnapping, had gained entrance to UN grounds in Geneva as member of nongovt orgn attending session on human-rights abuses, where Arundhati Ghose spotted him; thousands of such nongovt reprs enter UN headquarters in Geneva and New York each year; Switzerland automatically provides visas to members of groups accredited to UN (M)

1998-08-09.title: Rescuers and Investigators Sent by U.S. Begin to Arrive
1998-08-09.abstract: Pres Clinton vows that United States will not buckle under to threat of international terrorism; American Govt personnel arrive in East Africa to begin grim tasks of helping victims and opening investigation into nearly simultaneous bombings of United States Embassies in Kenya and Tanzania; American officials say that suspect at top of their list is Osama bin Laden, wealthy Saudi-born financier who this year renewed his vow to wage holy war against United States (M)

1998-08-09.title: The Living Search Among the Dead in Nairobi Mortuary
1998-08-09.abstract: Grieving people struggle throughout Nairobi to come to terms with what happened when terrorism strikes city unprepared for such a level of destruction and flood of thousands of victims into overwhelmed hospitals; they search hospitals for relatives, hoping they would find them alive, and file through morgues, anguishing over burned and badly disfigured remains, wondering if victims are family members or not (M)

1998-08-09.title: Abortion Debated in Chile, Where It's Always a Crime
1998-08-09.abstract: Chilean Senate takes up issue of abortion for first time, nine years after Gen Augusto Pinochet decreed that abortion is crime in Chilie even when necessary to save mother's life; advocates of women's rights say it is time for a change; bill, however, would only tighten regulations by increasing prison sentences for anyone convicted of performing an abortion; conservative opposition senators pushing bill say that want to crush 'abortion industry,' which they say is responsible for up to 160,000 clandestine abortions annually in country of 15 million people; photo of Sen Hernan Larrain (M)

1998-08-10.title: Now, a Push To Shore Up Ramparts At Embassies
1998-08-10.abstract: Senior Clinton Administration officials vow to track down terrorists who bombed American embassies Kenya and Tanzania, and call for expanded budget to fortify American diplomatic missions worldwide against growing threat from terrorists; Sec of State Madeleine K Albright says attacks, in which 12 Americans were killed, point up larger security problems at 280 American embassies and other diplomatic missions; repeats Pres Clinton's pledge to track down terrorists, no matter how long it might take; Bobby Ray Inman, retired admiral who led State Department panel that studied embassy security, says bombings show Department failed to meet security standards it supposedly adopted in mid-1980's; both embassies were not modified to comply fully with security standards called for by Inman's 1985 report, which was requested after 1984 attack against embassy in Beirut, Lebanon, in which 16 people died (M)

1998-08-10.title: Investigators in Tanzania Study Videocamera and Water Truck Belonging to the Embassy
1998-08-10.abstract: Videocamera at American Embassy in Dar Es Salaam, Tanzania, where terrorist bomb exploded on August 7 killing nine persons and injuring more than 70, may offer clues for investigators; US officials say camera was one of few pieces of equipment that remained intact in largely-devastated three-story building; say explosion appears to have been caused by bomb attached to car or truck (M)

1998-08-10.title: Their Quiet Lives, Rendered Stark and Bold by the Manner of Their Deaths
1998-08-10.abstract: Profile of Jesse Nathanael Aliganga, US Marine Corps master sergeant who died in bombing of American Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya; photo (S)

1998-08-10.title: Their Quiet Lives, Rendered Stark and Bold by the Manner of Their Deaths
1998-08-10.abstract: They hardly thought of themselves as heroes. Some were clerks, some were service personnel. Others were specialists in politics, health or international aid programs. But they were also people who loved music and dance, appreciated exotic lands and cultural differences, liked tending a garden or studying Swahili. They were sons and daughters, parents and siblings.    And yesterday the families, friends and colleagues of some of the 12 Americans who died in the bombing of the American Embassy in Kenya spoke of them as heroes of a sort: They had come from Georgia and Missouri, Indiana and Florida and other places, and they were in Nairobi as Americans who wanted to work for their country. As Kenneth R. Hobson Sr. said of his dead son: ''Anybody who wears the uniform is a hero.''

1998-08-10.title: Their Quiet Lives, Rendered Stark and Bold by the Manner of Their Deaths
1998-08-10.abstract: Profile of Arlene Kirk, US Air Force budget officer who died in bombing of US Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya; photo (S)

1998-08-11.title: U.S. Expects Ties to Improve Under Colombia's New President
1998-08-11.abstract: Clinton Administration says inauguration of Pres Andres Pastrana of Colombia should mean end of years of ill will between his nation and US, especially if he moves quickly on pledge to stem flood of Colombian cocaine into US (M)

1998-08-11.title: As Rescuers' Final Hopes Fade, Investigators' Toil Begins
1998-08-11.abstract: Rescuers dig through deep rubble of collapsed US Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya, in final, desperate search for victims of bombing; US law enforcement and intelligence teams begin sifting through scattered debris in equally painstaking search for tiniest clue that might provide evidence about culprits; photos (M)

1998-08-11.title: U.S. Won't Cower From Terrorism, Clinton Vows
1998-08-11.abstract: WASHINGTON—  President Bill Clinton on Monday mourned the victims of the bombings in Africa, vowed to "do whatever we can to bring the murderers to justice" and said the United States would "not be deterred by the threat of other action."

1998-08-11.title: Ex-Defense Minister of France Is Suspected of Money-Laundering
1998-08-11.abstract: Former French Defense Min Francois Leotard and his aide, Renaud Donnedieu de Vabres, have been formally placed under investigation on suspicion of taking part in money-laundering scheme to finance their political party (M)

1998-08-11.title: 10 Japanese In Hospital; 2d Poisoning Is Suspected
1998-08-11.abstract: Ten people in Niigata, Japan, are hospitalized after drinking tea and coffee made with hot water that appears to have been poisoned (M)

1998-08-12.title: Moscow Journal; For the Rolling Stones' Fans, Satisfaction at Last
1998-08-12.abstract: 50,000 fans jam Luzhniki stadium in Moscow to see Rolling Stones in their first concert in Russia; rock music was banned in Russia under Communists; Mick Jagger photo (M)

1998-08-12.title: Despite Loan Package, Russian Stocks and Bonds Fall Again
1998-08-12.abstract: Main index of Russian stocks falls 9.11 percent on Aug 10, nearly as great decline as on previous day, to its lowest level since May 1996; Government bonds fare even worse, falling to their lowest prices ever; analysts question whether Russia, already embarked on all-out effort to avert bankruptcy, will be able to rebuild confidence of world financial markets, which seem to have written it off (M)

1998-08-12.title: F.B.I. Sifts Vehicles' Scraps From Debris for Blast Clues
1998-08-12.abstract: FBI agents begin reconstructing hundreds of pieces of vehicles blown apart by powerful bomb at US Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya, in painstaking attempt to identify pickup truck that apparently carried explosives; workers find 18 more bodies, bringing death toll in Nairobi to 217; another 10 died in bombing in Tanzania; photos (M)

1998-08-12.title: 'Bunny' Breeds Rapidly, Sinking Belarus Economy
1998-08-12.abstract: Belarus's ruble has lost half its worth in five months, and more than 10 percent in last week alone; Government has announced new plan to shore up ruble's value; it will no longer accept its own currency as payment for exports; that will keep outsiders from pumping more rubles into economy already flooded with so many that currency is essentially worthless; outside experts say it will not solve real problem: Belarus Government insists on printing more rubles whenever it runs short of cash; photo (M)

1998-08-12.title: Police Detain 2 Over Spreading Internet Rumors : Virtual Riots in Malaysia
1998-08-12.abstract: KUALA LUMPUR—  Malaysian police on Tuesday detained two people suspected of spreading rumors over the Internet in a case that officials say highlights the fear of racial and social strife in a country battling its worst economic crisis in at least a decade.

1998-08-13.title: American Topics: Hagers-Town Almanack Offers Advice,Weather Forecasts and a Family Tradition
1998-08-13.abstract: It may not be quite as well-known as Poor Richard's Almanac, first published in 1733 by Benjamin Franklin, or as the Farmer's Almanac, but the Hagers-Town Almanack, a slice of pure Americana, has been published by the same family longer than any of its rivals.

1998-08-13.title: Toughening Stand on Weapons, Iraq Foils Long-Term Monitoring
1998-08-13.abstract: United Nations chief arms inspectors Richard Butler and Mohammed el-Baradei tell Security Council that Iraq is not only freezing all current arms inspections but also threatening long-term monitoring that is key to preventing nation from producing new weapons of mass destruction; cite Iraq's stand that inspectors will not be allowed to return to field to check on any suspected violations turned up by surveillance equipment during second phase of disarmament plan; say Iraq is also freezing technical talks on history of its arms production (M)

1998-08-13.title: Sophisticated Terrorists Pose Daunting Obstacle
1998-08-13.abstract: Intelligence officials say audacious timing and logistical skill needed to bomb American embassies in Nairobi, Kenya, and Dar es Salaam, Tanzania, nearly simultaneously indicates that foreign terrorist organizations are becoming increasingly complex and sophisticated; say Federal Bureau of Investigation and Central Intelligence Agency, cooperating on probes, face long, difficult effort against networks that are almost impossible to unravel (M)

1998-08-13.title: SWISS BANKS REACH HOLOCAUST ACCORD
1998-08-13.abstract: Swiss banks UBS and Credit Suisse agree to pay Holocaust survivors $1.25 billion in restitution to settle class-action suit charging banks retained assets of victims of Nazi era; Jewish groups and elected officials, including Sen Alfonse M D'Amato, hail accord; plaintiffs, as part of accord, agree to drop suit against Government-owned Swiss National Bank; deal also calls for state and local governments in United States to drop planned sanctions against Swiss (M)

1998-08-13.title: From Germany, Albright Escorts Home the Bodies of '10 Brave Americans'
1998-08-13.abstract: State Sec Madeleine K Albright escorts home from Germany bodies of 10 of 12 Americans killed in bombing of United States Embassy, Nairobi, Kenya; earlier visited Kenyan State Department employees and American staff member being treated at American military hospital, Landshut, Germany; met with Foreign Min Klaus Kinkel and thanked him for Germany's help (M)

1998-08-14.title: Rebels Closing In on Blacked-Out Congo Capital
1998-08-14.abstract: Rebels knock out all electrical power to Kinshasa, Congo, and tighten their hold on a strategic region outside the capital city; observers say that if rebels gain control of supply routes, capital could fall with little or no fighting; map; photo of new recruits being enlisted for Pres Laurent Kabila's forces (M)

1998-08-14.title: Clinton Tearfully Receives 10 Bodies, Praising Lives That 'Nothing Can Erase'
1998-08-14.abstract: Pres Clinton attends solemn ceremony at Andrews Air Force Base, Maryland, as bodies of 10 Americans killed in bombing of the American Embassy in Nairobi arrive home; victims were accompanied on flight from Germany by Secretary of State Madeleine K Albright; both Clinton and Albright comment; Clinton and Hillary Rodham Clinton meet privately with more than 60 of relatives in advance; those who died were cross section of foreign service community, including consul general, Army sergeant, security guard and epidemiologist with Centers for Disease Control; photos of members of family of bombing victims and of military honor guard (M)

1998-08-14.title: F.B.I. Reports Finding Pieces Of Bomb Truck
1998-08-14.abstract: Federal Bureau of Investigation reports that it has found fragments of truck that carried the explosives in bombing of American Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya; says picture of what happened in the seconds before bomb went off is being assembled; embassy's private guards have given FBI detailed account of what happened just before attack, and one guard helped FBI draw sketch of one of suspects; diagram mapping aspects of attack (M)

1998-08-14.title: Holocaust Accord Ends Plan for Sanctions
1998-08-14.abstract: Officials in New York, California, Pennsylvania and several other cities and states drop threatened sanctions against Swiss financial interests day after $1.25 billion settlement between Holocaust survivors and Swiss commercial banks accused of profiting from Nazi era officials; Jewish organizations and Holocaust survivors generally welcome bank settlment as at least partial closure on painful era; photo of Thomas Borer, head of Swiss task force on Holocaust-related issues, commenting on accord (M)

1998-08-14.title: U.S. to Change Strategy in Narcotics Fight in Colombia
1998-08-14.abstract: United States Government shifts course and agrees to help new administration of Pres Andres Pastrana in financing program to help Colombia's coca growers switch to legal crops; US had in past ruled out such aid for Colombia, citing distrust of previous administration of Pres Ernesto Samper and rebel dominance of coca-growing regions (M)

1998-08-15.title: Russia's Markets Rally as Western Powers Discuss Rescue
1998-08-15.abstract: Russia's stock and bond markets rally after Russia and major Western powers indicate they are working on another round of plans to end economic crisis; Pres Boris N Yeltsin insists he will not devalue ruble; has long phone conversation with Pres Clinton; banking system remains solvent, if short of foreign currency; Moscow banks open, but many refuse to sell dollars (M)

1998-08-15.title: Devastation From Explosion Permeates Kenyans' Lives
1998-08-15.abstract: Impact of American Embassy bombing, in which at least 247 Kenyans died and 5,000 were hurt, is spreading from Nairobi across nation; mourners gather at park where lists of dead and wounded are posted; many of dead who worked in and around embassy served as breadwinners for extended families; businesses are also suffering, with insurers refusing to cover blast losses; Pres Daniel arap Moi's reputation for corruption seen obstacle to reconstruction aid; photo (M)/

1998-08-15.title: Civilians Flee Congo's Capital in Fear of Rebel Advance
1998-08-15.abstract: Thousands of foreigners and Congolese flee Kinshasa, fearing rebel advance; jam airports; airlines cancel flights; United States, France, other Western nations arrange charter flights to evacuate remaining citizens; exodusfollows night without electricity or running water after rebels reportedly seize hydroelectric dam; Government restores power, but not running water; rebels say they have resumed flights of men and weapons from eastern cities toward west; photo (M)d

1998-08-15.title: C.I.A. Almost Bungled Intelligence Coup With Iraqi Refugee
1998-08-15.abstract: Central Intelligence Agency reportedly almost bungled intelligence coup when Dr Khidhir Abdul Abas Hamza, one of Iraq's top nuclear scientists, defected in 1994; Hamza fled to Libya in anger after CIA did not help him defect to United States; later arranged defection to US from Hungary after CIA smuggled his family out of Iraq; remains frustrated about unresolved immigration status and lack of green card (M)

1998-08-15.title: French Conservatives Split Over Extremists
1998-08-15.abstract: France's conservatives are reeling over decision to admit as member Jacques Blanc, regional assembly president who accepted support from extreme right to keep his post; Gilles de Robien, former leader of conservative umbrella group, cuts up party membership card on television (S)l

1998-08-16.title: Serbian Troops Step Up Terror in Kosovo
1998-08-16.abstract: Serbian policemen backed by Yugoslav Army soldiers are stepping up their terror against ethnic Albanian civilians in Kosovo, driving tens of thousands of them from their homes and shelling, looting and burning their villages; relief agencies estimate that up to 200,000 civilians have fled their homes since Government began military offensive against ethnic Albanian rebels on July 19; map; photo (M)

1998-08-16.title: End of a Dream in Kenya
1998-08-16.abstract: Two sisters who had planned to come to the United States were buried in their village eight days after a bomb ripped through the American Embassy in Nairobi.   Page 6.

1998-08-16.title: Foreign Exodus Leaves Kinshasa Unfazed
1998-08-16.abstract: US, Britain, France and other Western nations begin hurried final evacuation of their citizens in Congo amid signs that African-led negotiations to prevent rebel assault on Kinshasa will fail; Pres Laurent Kabila, who has been fighting for his political life against armed rebellion, has left Kinshasa and is in Lubumbashi; map; photo (M)

1998-08-16.title: Swiss Are Relieved, but Sour, Over Banks' Holocaust Accord
1998-08-16.abstract: Swiss are expressing mix of relief and victimization over announcement of accord aimed at settling Holocaust-era claims; many Swiss feel it is too late to undo damage that dispute has done to country's image (M)

1998-08-16.title: U.S. Sends Marines to Closed Embassy in Albania
1998-08-16.abstract: Company of marines arrives in Tirana, Albania, to guard US Embassy, which has been closed for fear of terrorist attack and its dependents and nonessential personnel sent home (M)

1998-08-17.title: Refugees In Kosovo Are in Peril
1998-08-17.abstract: Attacks by Yugoslav military forces against civilians in Serbian province of Kosovo continue, even as NATO begins military exercises intended to intimidate Yugoslav Govt into halting attacks, and despite intense diplomatic pressure by Western Governments; although rebels of Kosovo Liberation Army have been hurt in attacks, principal victims seem to be civilians; estimated 200,000 have fled homes in recent weeks and unknown number have been killed or wounded; attack against village of Lodja described; maps; photo (M)

1998-08-17.title: Big Italian Insurer Is Expected To Settle Claims on Holocaust
1998-08-17.abstract: US Sen Alfonse M D'Amato says holocaust survivors and Jewish charities are near agreement under which Generali, giant Italian insurance company, would pay at least $65 million to settle claims that it refused to honor policies of Eastern European Jews after World War II (S)

1998-08-17.title: U.S. Removes Its Diplomats From Embassy In Albania
1998-08-17.abstract: American diplomats, guarded by marines, are evacuated from Tirana, Albania, out of fears of possible terrorist attack on US Embassy by Islamic militants; worries are fueled not only by bombings of American Embassies in Kenya and Tanzania, but also by wave of arrests of Egyptians suspected of links to militant Islamic Jihad movement in Egypt; movement is linked to prime suspect in African bombings--Saudi financier Osama bin Laden (M)

1998-08-17.title: 2 Sides Prepare to Battle Over Congo's Capital
1998-08-17.abstract: Congolese forces loyal to Pres Laurent Kabila and rebel forces begin furious preparations for what may be critical fight for control of capital of Kinshasa, after several days of relative quiet; rebels are reportedly in firm control of strategic western port city of Matadi and nearby Inga Dam and are said to have cut off fuel and most food supplies to Kinshasa; are massing about 150 miles west of city; Government, vowing to fight to end, rushes 8,000 recently trained troops toward capital; Kabila, in last-minute effort to shore up positions, flies to Angola for meetings with Pres Jose Eduardo dos Santos; returns amid heavy security; maps; photo (M)

1998-08-17.title: U.N. Council Prepares Mild Reply to Iraq
1998-08-17.abstract: UN Security Council is preparing low-keyed response to Iraqi Pres Saddam Hussein's latest attempt to frustrate its arms inspectors; response reflects deep divisions among members over Council's approach to Iraq, as well as unwillingness of US and Britain to threaten military action as in February 1998 standoff; Council, faced with Iraq's defiant refusal to cooperate further with UN inspectors seeking to eliminate its weapons of mass destruction, will debate draft resolution acknowledging that plans to disarm Iraq have suffered setback, and instructing inspectors to press ahead with work and report any further Iraqi obstruction (M)

1998-08-18.title: What's Going to Happen To Russian Nest Eggs?
1998-08-18.abstract: Today's de facto devaluation of the ruble and rescheduling of government debt is supposed to spare the Russian Government financial ruin. Then there is Valeriya Yan, who is not being let off so easily.    Two years ago, Ms. Yan set a goal: to raise $24,000 so she and her husband could buy a two-room apartment for themselves and their 10-year-old son, away from the tiny one they share with two parents. She gave up teaching for a mindless but more lucrative job routing calls for a paging service for $300 a month.

1998-08-18.title: Denied Western Funds, Russia Makes Its Choices
1998-08-18.abstract: The Russian Government was denied additional financial help from the United States and several other allies over the weekend, causing it to take  steps the Clinton Administration had opposed: allowing the ruble greater fluctuation, imposing a moratorium on repaying foreign debt owed mostly by banks, and restructuring much of the Government's debt.    ''These particular choices were Russian choices in an exceptionally difficult environment,'' a senior Administration official said today. ''We certainly expressed concerns.''

1998-08-18.title: Flee Pakistan? Americans Wonder Why
1998-08-18.abstract: The American Ambassador told an anxious audience of some 150 Americans this afternoon that a charter plane would be arriving on Tuesday morning to evacuate dependents of embassy employees and other Americans who wanted to go.     ''It has to do with a range of threats against Americans and especially to American officials,'' Ambassador Thomas W. Simons Jr. said, explaining why embassy families had been ordered to depart and the State Department had urged all Americans ''to seriously consider leaving Pakistan.'' Although there was much skepticism among the audience about the seriousness of the threat, the fact that a charter plane was coming added to the tension among those faced with deciding whether to uproot their families the day the school year begins.

1998-08-18.title: 5 Held in Ulster Bombing as Some Identities Come to Light
1998-08-18.abstract: The British police arrested five men today in towns near this city for questioning on the car-bomb attack here on Saturday that killed 28 people and injured 220.    The blast, the deadliest in 29 years of sectarian violence in this predominantly Protestant British province, is widely believed to have been the work of a group that calls itself the Real I.R.A., a splinter group whose members left the Irish Republican Army this year.

1998-08-18.title: Controversy Over the Death Of a Serb War Crime Suspect
1998-08-18.abstract: A lawyer for a Bosnian Serb who died this month in a cell while being tried by the United Nations war crimes tribunal in The Hague bled to death because he did not get proper medical care, his lawyer says.    The lawyer, Dusan Vucicevic, said the tribunal was guilty of negligence and of causing the ''wrongful death'' of his client. He said he would demand that it pay damages to his client's family in Bosnia.

1998-08-19.title: Car Thefts Lead Israel To Wall Off West Bank
1998-08-19.abstract: A record number of car thefts has persuaded Israel's right-wing government to abandon its longtime opposition to  erecting any new barriers between Israel and the West Bank.    Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's Government had resisted the idea fiercely out of concern that such a line could be interpreted as a demarcation of Israel's future border. But it has shifted course in the face of public outcry over the disappearing cars, some 46,000 of which were stolen in Israel last year.

1998-08-19.title: Colombian Rebels Broaden Offensive With Attacks on Symbols of U.S. War Against Coca
1998-08-19.abstract: As leftist rebels occupied Albert Schweitzer Hospital in the city of Miraflores 10 days ago, using it as a vantage point to attack a military base across the street, explosions and gunfire rained over the  corrugated-tin roof of the hospital. A plane dropped bombs in the distance. Outside teen-agers fell dead and wounded.     ''How do you like our party?'' a witness recalled a guerrilla as having said.

1998-08-19.title: Rome Journal; The Pope and TV's 'Indelicate' Need for Planning
1998-08-19.abstract: Death, unlike taxes, is still certain in Italy. Even Popes must someday die. And when the Pope is as towering a figure as John Paul II, there is one other certainty: every major television news organization will someday need a room with a view.    More precisely, American networks will need a large terrace with a good view of St. Peter's Basilica.  CBS News recently outbid its rivals and signed a contract with the owner of the Atlante Star hotel for exclusive use of its 5,000-square-foot terrace overlooking the Vatican for live stand-ups by the anchor Dan Rather and others.

1998-08-19.title: Character Rating Sinks, Raising Questions About Ability to Lead Nation : 'I Misled People; I Deeply Regret That'
1998-08-19.abstract: WASHINGTON—  President Bill Clinton's televised admission of an inappropriate relationship with Monica Lewinsky brought a sharply critical response Tuesday from some Republicans and a sharp decline in the public's opinion of his character. But it left unanswered questions about the extent to which his ability to lead the nation has been compromised and where the investigation goes next.

1998-08-19.title: Top Fear in Congo Conflict: Wider Regional Violence
1998-08-19.abstract: By day's end here, when the sun's red disk sinks below the horizon and this electricity-deprived city fades to black, the only people who remain in the street are soldiers.    Clusters of men in uniform patrol on foot, clutching their rifles tightly, uncertain of their future as rebel forces threaten the capital, which is now on the razor's edge, and President Laurent Kabila's hold on power. And thousands of fresh recruits, still dressed in rags, mill about on streets whose names still date from the last fallen dictatorship.

1998-08-20.title: U.S. Warns Non-Muslims to Leave Afghanistan
1998-08-20.abstract: US Embassy in Pakistan urges all non-Muslim foreigners working in neighboring Afghanistan to leave country because of fears for their safety; warning compounds fears and tensions generated by bombings of embassies in Kenya and Tanzania; coalition of militant Islamic organizations in Cairo threaten further attacks on Americans; group, World Islamic Front for Holy War Against Jews and Crusaders, is part of far-reaching network run by Osama bin Laden, Islamic militant who has publicly threatened holy war against US and who operates from bases in Afghanistan; leader of Afghanistan's ruling Taliban denies that bin Laden had any role in bombings (M)

1998-08-20.title: Ulster Buries Bomb Victims, Fearful for Peace Accord
1998-08-20.abstract: Funerals are held in Omagh, Northern Ireland, for 16 victims of Aug 15 car bomb; mourners heap scorn on Republican die-hards responsible for violence; revulsion is only increased by news that splinter group, Real IRA, has suddenly called cease-fire after earlier admitting responsibility for blast; some funerals are attended by Gerry Adams, Sinn Fein leader, and David Trimble, Protestant Unionist leader; photo (M)

1998-08-20.title: As Rebels Near, Congo's President Scrambles for Support
1998-08-20.abstract: Pres Laurent Kabila of Congo scrambles to obtain foreign military support to repel final assault by rebels closing in capital of Kinshasa; wins pledges of military and other support from Zimbabwe, Kenya and Angola; is unable to win larger political cover of group of 14 African nations; photo; maps (M)

1998-08-20.title: Hotel Janitors Don't Recall Bomb Materials in Kenya
1998-08-20.abstract: Hilltop Lodge, low-cost hotel in run-down neighborhood of Nairobi, Kenya, which is popular with Muslim executives, is focus of investigation into bombing of US Embassy on August 7; FBI agents are combing rooms that three suspects rented there (M)

1998-08-20.title: Vancouver Journal; A Laid-Back Place Overdoses on Drug Culture
1998-08-20.abstract: Vancouver, British Columbia, is gaining reputation as haven for ilicit drugs and those who use them as result of brazen addicts on streets and enormous amount of high-potency marijuana raised, sold and smoked openly; Cannabis Cafe, which celebrates marijuana, is drug mecca for many west coast Americans, but public-relations nightmare for city; photo (M)

1998-08-21.title: Except for Israel, Mideast Is Notably Silent on Strikes
1998-08-21.abstract: Israel is quick to congratulate US for missile attacks on suspected terrorist bases in Afghanistan and Sudan, but early reaction from many other governments in Middle East has been silence; only Libya and Iraq, declared US foes, are explicit in condemnation; Britain is alone among European leaders in expressing support; Japan gives qualified backing; Israelis, who have suffered more recent terrorist attacks than any other country, sees US strikes as vindication of their own calls for controntation toward Islamic militants (M)

1998-08-21.title: Man With Mission Takes On the U.S. At Far-Flung Sites
1998-08-21.abstract: Profile of Osama bin Laden, exiled Saudi billionaire who from Afghan redoubt has declared war on American interests everywhere; rage is fueled by American power in Middle East and has backing of international network of terrorists and companies; photo (M)

1998-08-21.title: A New Worldwide Warning For Americans to Be Cautious
1998-08-21.abstract: US issues new worldwide warning to Americans and diplomatic personnel to exercise much greater caution than usual, following cruise missile attacks against suspected terrorist targets in Afghanistan and Sudan; steps by US to remove nonessential diplomatic employees and families from vulnerable embassies following August 7 bombings in Kenya and Tanzania discussed (M)

1998-08-21.title: Arvidsjaur Journal; Sweden's Frozen North Can Be Balmy for Business
1998-08-21.abstract: Swedish Government, in effort to slow flight of people from sparsely settled area near Arctic Circle, is urging businesses to set up computerized telephone-call centers in remote villages, such as Arvidsjaur, and to staff them with young people who would otherwise be scouting for work in population centers in south; photo (M)

1998-08-21.title: CRITICS OF CLINTON SUPPORT ATTACKS
1998-08-21.abstract: Most of Pres Clinton's political opponents back decision to launch missile attacks against terrorist bases in Afghanistan and Sudan, underscoring cherished axiom of American foreign policy that politics stops at water's edge, but some question his timing because of White House sex scandal; most Republican Congressional leaders, such as Speaker Newt Gingrich and Sen Jesse Helms, are supportive, but others, such as Sen Dan Coats says Clinton's loss of credibility because of sex scandal casts doubt on his actions and motives (M)

1998-08-22.title: Mandela Says Congo's Neighbors Will Hold Talks on Revolt
1998-08-22.abstract: As countries entangled in civil war in Congo threaten to turn nation into battlefield for divergent ethnic, political and diplomatic rivalries, Pres Nelson Mandela of South Africa announces plans to weekend talks with leaders involved; aides to Pres Laurent Kabila say they might participate in negotiations (M)

1998-08-22.title: U.S. Says Raids Worked and May Stall Terror Attacks
1998-08-22.abstract: Clinton Adm makes measured claims of success for cruise-missile attacks in Sudan and Afghanistan; officials say attack on mountain redoubt in Afghanistan will disrupt training and, possibly, future attacks by terrorists sponsored by Osama bin Laden; cloudy weather and haze obscure satellite photographs of what Adm describes as sprawling terrorist training camp in Afghanistan; senior Adm officials say preliminary assesments indicate that missiles destroyed or damaged many buildings in complex about 90 miles south of Kabul; Pres Clinton signs exec order that places bin Laden on Treasury Dept's list of terrorists and their sponsors, clearing way for officials to seize his assets; photo of wreckage of pharmaceutical factory in Khartoum, Sudan (L)

1998-08-22.title: Fire Sale of Asian Assets Undermines Economies In Russia and Venezuela : Stock Markets Drop Across Europe and The Americas
1998-08-22.abstract: NEW YORK—  Stock prices crumbled in Europe and the Americas on Friday as deflationary pressures from Asia infected other emerging markets, notably Russia and Venezuela.

1998-08-22.title: Two Senators Say Evidence Of Plotting Is Compelling
1998-08-22.abstract: Pres Clinton's top national security advisers begin showing small group of lawmakers evidence they have to justify missile attacks on terrorist targets; lawmkers interviewed say they agree that case against Osama bin Laden is as compelling as Adm contends; Sen Gordon H Smith, Oregon Republican on Foreign Relations Commitee, and Sen Charles S Robb, Virginia Democrat on Select Committee on Intelligence, say Adm officials had laid out 'irrefutable' evidence from variety of sources; table, map shows links between bin Laden and various terrorist activities (M)

1998-08-22.title: AFTER THE ATTACKS: THE REACTION -- U.S. Raids Provoke Fury in Muslim World; Russia Is Critical
1998-08-22.abstract: Russia denounces United States for its cruise-missile attacks on Afghanistan and Sudan; but it also declares attacks will have no affect on Pres Clinton's trip to Moscow 10 days from now (S)

1998-08-23.title: Missile Strikes Are Seen As New Strategy for U.S.
1998-08-23.abstract: US and Western officials say Clinton Administration's decision to use missile strikes against suspected terrorist facilities in Afghanistan and Sudan, in retaliation for bombings of American embassies in Kenya and Tanzania, is significant departure from past practice of seeking international support and UN authorization; say Clinton was less inclined to wait and allow US to again appear weak or irresolute, citing ambivalence of Muslim allies, UN delay in dealing with Iraq and mounting evidence of plans for impending attacks on more American embassies (M)

1998-08-23.title: PRESIDENT SWEARS TO USE 'ALL TOOLS' AGAINST TERRORISM
1998-08-23.abstract: Pres Clinton says US will use 'all the tools at our disposal' to fight terrorist network of Osama bin Laden, as Administration outlines efforts to squeeze him financially after US cruise-missile strikes against sites linked to him in Afghanistan and Sudan; signs executive order calling for Treasury Dept to block any financial transactions with American companies by bin Laden and his principal terrorist organization, Islamic Army (M)

1998-08-23.title: Utah Struggles With a Revival of Polygamy
1998-08-23.abstract: Modern Utah still struggles with issue of polygamy, which was brought to territory by Mormon pioneers and persists in small pockets even though it has long since been outlawed, and polygamists have been excommunicated by Mormon Church for more than a century; estimated 10,000 people now live in polygamous families; current debate is led by Tapestry of Polygamy, self-help group of former polygamous wives, and Women's Religious Liberties Union, whose members defend their polygamous relationships; photos; issue erupts with case of 16-year-old girl who stumbled into remote gas station this summer, covered in bruises inflicted by her father, who was trying to force her to become her uncle's 15th wife; John Daniel Kingston, leader of wealthy, secretive clan, is charged with abuse; Rowenna Erickson, who left clan in 1991, says incest, child marriage and birth defects are becoming more frequent among 1,500 members; she says Kingston fathered ten children with his half-sister (M)

1998-08-23.title: Unhappy as a 'Pariah,' Sudanese Say U.S. Picked the Wrong Target
1998-08-23.abstract: Sudanese vent full fury at US for missile attacks on suspected terrorist facilities, in retaliation for bombings of American embassies in Kenya and Tanzania; thousands stage anti-American demonstrations in capital of Khartoum; photo (M)

1998-08-23.title: Father of Nairobi Bomb Victim Says Clinton Kept His Promise
1998-08-23.abstract: Kenneth Hobson Sr, resident of Lamar, Mo, whose son, Army Sgt Kenneth R Hobson Jr, died in terrorist bombing of US Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya, says Pres Clinton fulfilled promise to victims' families with retaliatory missile strikes against suspected terrorist sites in Afghanistan and Sudan (M)

1998-08-24.title: South Africa's Role
1998-08-24.abstract: News analysis of South Africa's unsuccessful efforts to mediate peaceful resolution of conflict in Congo; finds minimal results of efforts by Pres Nelson Mandela shows South Africa is still struggling to find its footing among neighboring nations, many of which remain suspicious and resentful of country's economic power and international adulation accorded Mandela (M)

1998-08-24.title: Yeltsin Dismisses Russian Government
1998-08-24.abstract: Russian Pres Boris Yeltsin dismisses five-month-old team of economic reformers and reappoints Viktor S Chernomyrdin as Prime Min in anxious bid to shore up political support; reappointment of Chernomyrdin and ouster of Sergei V Kiriyenko comes months after he derided Chenomyrdin as spent force and cast him out of Government; abrupt action raises fresh doubts about Kremlin's commitment to tough economic reforms and poses unsettling questions about Yeltsin's ability to govern; while Chernomyrdin is seen by most of Russian public as uninspired, he has been consensus figure for much of power elite; financial oligarchy regards him as far more sympathetic and pliable than Kiriyenko--even though such attributes make him questionable choice to extract Russia from its economic quagmire; photos (M)

1998-08-24.title: On Nairobi's Front Pages, a War of Rumors
1998-08-24.abstract: Even as officials release little information on investigation into August 7 terrorist bombing of US Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya, welter of untrue, unconfirmed and tantalizing reports about suspects and leads in case have been appearing in Kenyan newspapers; photo (M)

1998-08-24.title: CONGO RECAPTURES A STRATEGIC BASE
1998-08-24.abstract: Congolese Govt forces and allies recapture strategic military base at Kitona from rebels, possibly halting their drive toward capital of Kinshasa; rebels appear to have seized Kisingani, country's third largest city, solidifying positions in eastern Congo; developments raise possibility of protracted war, in which Government and rebels, backed by respective allies, would fight each other from entrenched positions in country's second civil war in two years; military advances overtake diplomatic efforts, led by Pres Nelson Mandela of South Africa, to negotiate peaceful resolution; maps; photo (M)

1998-08-24.title: Despite Confusion, Clinton Will Keep Date With Yeltsin
1998-08-24.abstract: Clinton Administration is concerned over dismissal of reformist Cabinet by Russian Pres Boris Yeltsin so close to September 1 summit with Pres Clinton in Moscow; officials acknowledge they were taken by surprise, but say move will not affect trip; comments by former national security adviser Zbigniew Brzezinski and other prominent Russian scholars noted (M)

1998-08-25.title: Military in Indonesia Ousts Senior General
1998-08-25.abstract: The Indonesian military, pressing ahead with a risky housecleaning that has involved exhuming bodies and investigating high-ranking officers, today discharged a son-in-law of former President Suharto.    One of Mr. Suharto's most feared generals, the son-in-law, Lieut. Gen. Prabowo Subianto, symbolized the army's repressive nature.

1998-08-25.title: U.S. and Iraq Toughen Stances on Weapons Inspection
1998-08-25.abstract: US and Iraq harden their positions over Baghdad's decision to end cooperation with weapons inspections; US and Britain have begun pressing for end to periodic sanctions reviews; Iraq insists on more friendly inspection system and perhaps another visit, or new initiative, from Sec Gen Kofi Annan, bypassing Security Council (M)

1998-08-25.title: Border Deaths Don't Change Mexico's View Of Crossings
1998-08-25.abstract: Recent deaths of 10 Mexicans who were found in California desert have drawn attention to Mexico's longstanding position of not discouraging its citizens from undertaking illegal--and increasingly dangerous--journey across border; over 90 Mexicans trying to emigrate illegally have been found dead in California desert so far this year (M)

1998-08-25.title: Burmese Opposition Leader Ends Latest 13-Day Roadside Protest
1998-08-25.abstract: Myanmar opposition leader, Daw Aung San Suu Kyi, returns to her home in Yangon after staying in her car for 13 days, surrounded by soldiers who blocked her from traveling to meet supporters; her political standoff with military Goverment continues as she presses her demand for convening of Parliament, dominated by her party, that was elected in 1991 but was never allowed to take office (M)

1998-08-25.title: A Moderate Thinks U.S. Shot Itself in the Foot
1998-08-25.abstract: Sudanese scholar Abdulrahman Abuzayd says US made serious missteps by failing to convince Muslim world that Osama bin Laden was responsible for bombings of US embassies in East Africa and then by attacking Afghanistan and Sudan (M)

1998-08-26.title: Egyptians Hold Terrorist Chief, Official Asserts
1998-08-26.abstract: Arab intelligence official says Palestinian guerrilla chief, Sabri al-Banna, better known as Abu Nidal, is being held by Egyptian authorities in Cairo; US has accused Abu Nidal, one of world's most wanted terrorists, of having killed or injured 900 people in attacks in 20 countries since 1974; he is wanted in US, Great Britain and Italy and was condemned to death by Palestine Liberation Organization, from which he broke in 1972; details of his capture are unclear; Abu Nidal photo (M)

1998-08-26.title: Serbian Cannon Fire Kills 3 Mother Teresa Aid Workers
1998-08-26.abstract: Three ethnic Albanians working for Mother Teresa relief organization are killed by cannon fire from police vehicle in village of Vlaski Drenovac, Serbia; attack appears to be Yugoslav Government's most serious effort yet to terrorize aid workers; photo (M)

1998-08-26.title: British Leader Acts to Tighten Terrorism Laws
1998-08-26.abstract: Prime Min Tony Blair visits Omagh, Northern Ireland, and announces that he is calling Parliament back into session from its summer recess to toughen antiterrorism laws; similar measures are being contemplated by Irish Republic after bombing in Omagh that took 28 lives and wounded 220 people (M)

1998-08-26.title: Cuban Exile Leader Among 7 Accused of Plot on Castro
1998-08-26.abstract: Jose Antonio Llama, member of board of Cuban-American National Foundation, is indicted in San Juan, PR, with six other men on charges that they conspired to assassinate Pres Fidel Castro of Cuba when he was on official visit to Venezuela in 1997; others indicted are Jose Rodriguez Sosa, Alfredo Otero, Angel Alfonso Aleman, Angel Hernandez Rojo, Juan Bautista Marquez and Francisco Secundino Cordova; indictment does not mention foundation, principal Cuban exile organization in US; photos (M)

1998-08-26.title: China Admits Ecological Sins Played Role in Flood Disaster
1998-08-26.abstract: Charges are mounting in Beijing that bad policies and official neglect have worsened this summer's disastrous flooding in China; Government has made unusual admission that its land-use mistakes are partly to blame and announced sweeping policy changes; officials have been shocked by vast scale of damage in southern and northeastern China, where severe flooding persists; official press has carried articles and editorials on harmful effects of clear-cutting timber along Yangtze River; Zhao Qizheng, chief of State Council Information Office, says Government has decided to shut down logging activities in upper catchments of Yangtze River; says all cleared areas will be replanted in long-term strategy of ecological restoration; this year's floods have revived private debate about Three Gorges Dam under construction along upper reaches of Yangtze; map; photo (M)

1998-08-27.title: Francesco Crucitti, Pope's Personal Surgeon, Dies at 67
1998-08-27.abstract: Dr Francesco Crucitti, who performed emergency surgery on Pope John Paul II after an assassination attempt in 1981 and went on to become his personal surgeon, dies at age of 67; Pope pays homage to 'man who saved my life'; photo (M)

1998-08-27.title: Possible Benign Use Is Seen for Chemical At Factory in Sudan
1998-08-27.abstract: Organization for Prohibition of Chemical Weapons, international agency overseeing treaty that bars chemical weapons, says chemical that US cited to justify its missile attacks on Sudanese factory can be used for commercial products; US has insisted that chemical found outside plant could only mean that plant was intended to make nerve agent VX; treaty group concedes that it is not aware of any commercial product on market that contains chemical, known as Empta; defense and intelligence officials say they still believe there is no other explanation for presence of Empta in soil sample secretly collected outside factory several months ago; photo (M)

1998-08-27.title: New Delhi Journal; Trying to Wreck India's Romance With the Bomb
1998-08-27.abstract: Article on Arundhati Roy, internationally acclaimed novelist from India who has criticized her nation for testing nuclear weapons; map; photo (M)

1998-08-27.title: American Inspector on Iraq Quits, Accusing U.N. and U.S. of Cave-In
1998-08-27.abstract: William S Ritter Jr, longest-serving US weapons inspector in Iraq, resigns, charging that Sec Gen Kofi Annan, Security Council and Clinton Administration have stymied inspectors on 'doorstep' of uncovering Iraq's hidden weapons programs; says in his resignation letter that failure to push aggressively ahead with inspections is 'surrender to Iraqi leadership' that makes 'farce' of commission's efforts to prove that Iraq is concealing chemical, biological and nuclear weapons programs; asserts that between Nov 1997 and Aug 1998, Administration made at least seven efforts to delay or stop investigation or block line of inquiry; makes his resignation letter public in effort to force US and United Nations to return to tougher stance; Ritter photo (M)

1998-08-27.title: Congolese Troops Battle Rebels on Kinshasa's Outskirts
1998-08-27.abstract: Congolese soldiers and rebel forces clash on outskirts of Kinshasa in most intense fighting near capital since rebellion began on Aug 2; it is unclear whether Government has succeeded in crushing rebels' last-ditch attempt to seize Kinshasa; map (M)

1998-08-28.title: Korea Slump 'Full-Blown' After 6.6% Quarterly Fall : For Asians, Recession Reality
1998-08-28.abstract: SEOUL—  The battered economy of South Korea shrank at a 6.6 percent rate in the second quarter, pushing it officially into recession, the Bank of Korea said Thursday. It was the worst quarter since 1980.

1998-08-28.title: Congo's War Turns to Brutal Killings on City's Streets
1998-08-28.abstract: Congo Govt tightens security in Kinshasa, even as fighting just outside capital diminishes; evidence emerges that civilians and soldiers had brutally killed several suspected rebel infiltrators; photos of soldiers executing suspected rebel (M)

1998-08-28.title: Blast Suspect Held in U.S. And Is Said to Admit Role
1998-08-28.abstract: Federal authorities bring suspect in bombings of United States Embassies in Kenya and Tanzania to United States, saying that he had admitted that he was trained at terror camps in Afghanistan run by Osama bin Laden, Saudi-born multimillionaire suspectged of having inspired, organized and financed bombings; suspect, Mohamed Rashed Daoud al-'Owhali, arrives in New York and appears in court; another suspect, Mohammed Saddiq Odeh, who provided one of first breaks in case, is expected to arrive in New York on August 28; al-'Owhali is said to have acknowleged that he rode as passenger in truck that carried massive bomb to embassy in Nairobi; photos of suspects and FBI Dir Louis Freeh, with State Sec Madeleine Albright and national security adviser Samuel Berger (M)

1998-08-28.title: Excerpts From Court Charges: To Terrorists, 'It Was Proper to Kill Americans'
1998-08-28.abstract: Excerpts from charges against Mohamed Rashed Daoud al-'Owhali as filed in Federal District Court (M)

1998-08-28.title: Congressional Leaders Plan Hearings on Iraq Arms Monitoring
1998-08-28.abstract: Senior Republicans on House and Senate foreign affairs committees say they will hold hearings to determine whether Clinton Adm has weakened inspection efforts by United Nations Special Commission directed to disarm Iraq of biological, chemical and nuclear weapons; Sen Sam Brownback and Repr Benjamin A Gilman say they are deeply troubled by allegations by William S Ritter, senior American inspector who resigned in protest of Adm policies and Security Council inaction on Iraq (M)

1998-08-29.title: Russia Told to Implement Tough I.M.F. Reform Plan
1998-08-29.abstract: Finance ministers of Germany, France, Italy and Britain warn Russia that the only way out of its current crisis is to make the tough economic changes long urged by International Monetary Fund, including enforcing tax laws and hewing to a monetary policy that keeps inflation in check (M)

1998-08-29.title: Government Said to Gain Upper Hand in Congo's Capital
1998-08-29.abstract: Two days after heavy shelling and gunfire erupted on fringes of Kinshasa, Governments forces are reported to be regaining control of the capital, although rebels still hold other parts of the country; photo (M)

1998-08-29.title: Gingrich Opposes Summit, but President Vows to Press On
1998-08-29.abstract: House Speaker Newt Gingrich urges cancellation of Pres Clinton's planned meeting with Pres Boris Yeltsin in Moscow; Clinton vows to go ahead with visit and is supported by Sens Daniel Patrick Moynihan, John McCain and others; photo (M)

1998-08-29.title: Russian Emigrants: Mixed Views on the Old Country
1998-08-29.abstract: Free fall of Russian economy is met with alarm and also apathy in Brighton Beach, a Russian enclave in Brooklyn; some rush to wire money to relatives in Russia, while others voice no concern; photos (M)

1998-08-29.title: I.M.F. Chief Endorses Chernomyrdin's Goals
1998-08-29.abstract: Michel Camdessus, managing director of International Monetary Fund, supports Russia's acting Prime Minister, Viktor S Chernomyrdin, in his efforts to resurrent an austerity plan for Russia; says that if Russians fail to embrace the fund's approach, it would be fruitless for the fund or the major industrial nations to lend more money in an attempt to support the ruble and the Russian economy (M)

1998-08-30.title: Poor Face Grim Choices as Pakistan's Economy, and Government, Unravel
1998-08-30.abstract: Photo of Muscovites carting off the goods they bought in anticipation of further decline in value of ruble

1998-08-30.title: Ruble's Crash Sets Off Boom in Survival Strategies
1998-08-30.abstract: Collapse of ruble sets off boom in survival strategies among Russians, affecting everyone, from ordinary citizens to small business owners; many shops have raised prices or simply closed while citizens struggle to withdraw money from banks; many who kept savings under mattresses have gone on wild buying sprees, looking for ways to safely park money before value declines any further; photos (M)

1998-08-30.title: A Sacred Indian Site, but Still the Font of Strife
1998-08-30.abstract: Hindu-Moslem distrust runs deep in India over plan to build a temple to Ram in Ayodhya on the site of a 16th-century Babri Masjid mosque, which was demolished by Hundu militants in 1992; Hindu nationalist Bharatiya Janata Party, which came to power on pledge to build the temple, has since pledged to build it only if the courts give permission and nation's laws are changed to allow it; photos; map (M)

1998-08-30.title: Mexico's Rookie Deputies Hope for Better Season
1998-08-30.abstract: Lower house of Mexico's Congress records major accomplishments in the year since it came under opposition control; formerly discredited body is now set on a new democratic path, one in which presidential initiatives are no longer blindly passed, where debate is vigorous and where the art of compromise is being refined; transformation is not complete, and governing party is still in control of Senate; photos (M)

1998-08-30.title: Paris Adds a Garden to Diana's Thriving Memorials
1998-08-30.abstract: City of Paris decides to create a children's garden in memory of Diana, Princess of Wales, in Marais district; Place de 'Alma, which is directly above the tunnel in which Diana died, now serves as unofficial but very popular monument to her; investigations into fatal crash continue; photo (M)

1998-08-31.title: Remembering Diana, but Moving On
1998-08-31.abstract: LONDON—  The flowers are back, glazed under cellophane, laid at the railings of Kensington Palace and bearing the familiar moving or mawkish messages.

1998-08-31.title: Rebel Terror Forcing Minority Serbs Out of Kosovo
1998-08-31.abstract: Foreign diplomats and human rights workers say ethnic Albanian rebels have begun wave of kidnappings and terror intended to drive out small Serbian population of Kosovo Province; nearly every ethnic Serbian family that has lived in areas controlled by rebels has fled or disappeared; more than 80 civilians are missing, and human rights workers believe they have been killed; photo; map (M)

1998-08-31.title: U.S. Officials Wary on Moscow, Fearing Backsliding on Economic Changes
1998-08-31.abstract: Clinton Administration warily monitors maneuvering in Russia to form new Government; is disturbed by possible signs of backsliding on commitment to tough economic changes but reluctant to render verdict until new personnel and policies are in place (M)

1998-08-31.title: A Subdued Anniversary at a Place Dedicated to Diana
1998-08-31.abstract: Britons have shed some of saintly adulation of Diana, Princess of Wales, in the year since her death; prepartions for anniversary have been muted, reflecting what some newspapers call 'Diana fatigue,' even as they, like most television stations, fill columns and air time with memorials to her; photo (M)

1998-08-31.title: Albright in Bosnia Urging More Reconcilation Efforts
1998-08-31.abstract: Sec of State Madeleine K Albright spends night in Sarajevo, Bosnia, in what American officials call dramatic symbol of the distance Bosnia has come since US intervention ended Balkan conflict in 1995; presses Serbs, Croats and Muslims to make still-artificial and bitterly divided Bosnia closer to multi-ethnic democracy envisioned in Dayton Accords; openly campaigns Biljana Plavsic, Bosnia Serb president, and other candidates in upcoming elections who are considered supporters of Dayton Accord; photo (M)
